text: The work herein presents our findings regarding mechanistic events associated with the alkene hydrofunctionalization that utilizes 9-mesityl-10-methylacridinium (Mes-Acr(+)) as the photoredox catalyst. We consistently observe the alkene cation radical as well as find that phenylthiyl (PhSH) can rapidly oxidize the acridinyl radical through a union of photoredox and hydrogen atom transfer (HAT) catalysis events. Also, we find that the workhorse, diphenyl disulfide ((PhS)2), catalyzes the reaction similarly to the thiophenol through the action of the disulfide bond being photolytically cleaved. DFT studies that focus on transition structures reveal the ease of which H atoms are donated to alkenes by PhSH compared to the hydrogen source of 2-phenylmalononitrile (PMN) as a result of some significant structure rearrangement. Furthermore, a large number of observations of Mes-Acr(+) engaging in nonproductive adducts that form within the catalyst solution prior to any conversion suggest the presence of buildup of the PhS(-) species over time along with the kinetic differences of HAT activities of PhSH and (PhS)2 reactions.
explanation: Highly domain-specific and coherent, with plausible experimental details and terminology typical of chemistry research; this reduces the likelihood of AI-generated text, though a capable AI could emulate this style.
